body.title: [sprint] Rename "lever" -> "proof method" and define it in rules/ — the metaphor of force caused a real conflation
body.intention: I intend that the mechanism which makes a CLASS of claim provable has an inert, precise name and a definition in rules/, so that it cannot be confused with a single proof or with the proof corpus that earns a gate's removal.
body.description:
Akien, 2026-07-09: 'what means levers here?' then 'maybe we just need a different term to make it easier for you to catch it?'

**THE TERM CAUSED A REAL ERROR, MEASURED.** CC wrote the contract line 'The gates are scaffolding; the missing-levers backlog earns their removal.' That is FALSE. It welds CLAUDE.md's sentence ('the accumulated missing-levers are the backlog that earns the gates' eventual removal') onto Akien's stricter statement of 2026-06-21 ('the PROOF CORPUS is what earns the right to remove a gate... Removing a gate without accumulated proof is just hoping — it reintroduces the hollow build we're curing (CP1). No gate comes off on optimism.'), skipping the two middle steps.

The real chain: name the missing proof method -> build it -> the CLASS becomes provable -> proofs accumulate -> the proof corpus earns the gate's removal. The backlog is the MAP of what is unprovable. The corpus is the PAYMENT.

**WHY THE WORD DID IT.** 'Lever' is a metaphor of force — levers move things — so the sentence reads as causally complete and the metaphor silently supplies the missing step. An inert noun cannot. 'Proof method' is standard, unglamorous, and drags the missing word CLASS back into every sentence: you cannot write 'the missing-proof-methods backlog earns gate removal' without hearing that a method earns nothing.

**THREE THINGS, TWO WORDS.** proof = one instance. lever = the mechanism that makes a class provable. proof corpus = the accumulation that earns a gate's removal. The middle one is the only one with a bad name, and the third has NO name. That is why the second collapsed into the third.

**IT IS DEFINED NOWHERE.** grep: 'lever' appears in no rules/ artifact. Its only definition anywhere is a subordinate clause in CLAUDE.md line 138 — 'the lever that makes the class provable'. Undefined jargon in the one document that arbitrates conflicts is how it drifted.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/_builder_close.py (missing_lever param + docstring), unseen_university/ticket_status.py (comment), 15 code sites total by grep; CLAUDE.md (6 lines); skills/ (4 files); a NEW rules/ artifact defining proof-method / proof / proof-corpus.
**Design rules:** annotate-never-delete (CP2) — the 227 historical memory-store files mentioning 'lever' are HISTORY and are NOT rewritten; canonical memory home devlab/runtime/memory/; rules artifacts carry statement + why (see T-rules-store-materialize).
**Scope boundary:** IN — rename the code identifier missing_lever -> missing_proof_method with a back-compat read of the old key; update CLAUDE.md and the 4 skills; emit a rules/ artifact defining all THREE terms and their causal chain. OUT — rewriting the 227 historical store records; renaming 'red form' (that is the shape of ONE proof and must stay distinct); building any proof method (T-mutation-red-retroactive-proof is the first one).
**Test plan:** (a) grep proves zero remaining `missing_lever` identifiers in code, and that reading a ticket closed with the OLD key still resolves (back-compat). (b) A rules_citation_check-style test asserts the proof-method rule artifact exists and resolves. (c) The discriminating test: assert the rules artifact states that the PROOF CORPUS earns gate removal and the BACKLOG does not — a presence-check on the word 'proof method' would pass on template-dressed vagueness, so the node must assert the causal claim. Red form: no rules artifact exists; the term is defined nowhere.
**Completion criteria:** `grep -rn missing_lever --include=*.py` returns 0; a ticket closed with the old key still reads back; rules/ carries a proof-method artifact defining proof vs proof-method vs proof-corpus and stating that the corpus, not the backlog, earns gate removal.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.completion_criteria: Zero missing_lever identifiers in code with back-compat read. CLAUDE.md + 4 skills updated. A rules/ artifact defines proof / proof-method / proof-corpus and states the corpus earns gate removal, not the backlog. No historical store record rewritten.
